channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
item.description: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.
item.content:encoded:
A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.

(Image credit: Nicole Tung)
item.dc:creator: Nicole Tung

item.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
item.description: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

(Image credit: Tang Ke/VCG via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Judge halts local TV giant Nexstar's takeover of rival Tegna until trial
item.description: U.S. Judge Trevor Nunley ruled that consumers could suffer irreparable harm if Nexstar integrated Tegna's stations into its own operations ahead of an antitrust trial.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Judge Trevor Nunley ruled that consumers could suffer irreparable harm if Nexstar integrated Tegna's stations into its own operations ahead of an antitrust trial.

(Image credit: Joe Sohm/Visions of America)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Gasoline could drop below $4 in coming days
item.description: Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: NPR receives $113 million in charitable gifts
item.description: "My hope is that this commitment provides the stability and the spark NPR needs to innovate boldly and strengthen its national network," says Connie Ballmer, who gave $80 million of the $113 million.
item.content:encoded:
"My hope is that this commitment provides the stability and the spark NPR needs to innovate boldly and strengthen its national network," says Connie Ballmer, who gave $80 million of the $113 million.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: A Polymarket trader made $300,000 betting on Biden's pardons, a new analysis shows
item.description: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.content:encoded: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Jet fuel prices double, leading airlines to increase baggage fees, raise fares
item.description: Airlines are facing higher costs, and one airport group in Europe has warned of the risk of a "systemic jet fuel shortage" if traffic through the Strait of Hormuz doesn't normalize by the end of this month.
item.content:encoded:
Airlines are facing higher costs, and one airport group in Europe has warned of the risk of a "systemic jet fuel shortage" if traffic through the Strait of Hormuz doesn't normalize by the end of this month.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Jet fuel supplies are sharply affected by the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The reduction in ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has had an outsize impact on global jet fuel supplies. Prices have doubled.
item.content:encoded: The reduction in ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has had an outsize impact on global jet fuel supplies. Prices have doubled.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Visionary NPR leader Kevin Klose, who led network to new heights, has died
item.description: Klose led NPR for a decade starting in 1998, a period of incredible growth for the public media network.
item.content:encoded:
Klose led NPR for a decade starting in 1998, a period of incredible growth for the public media network.

(Image credit: Jacques Coughlin/Jacques Coughlin)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Once again, Trump threatens to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell
item.description: President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
